notes: Rebuilt from 5 insight files (up from 1). The four new discovery interviews (Alysa, Anna, Molly, Monika) enable cross-call frequency weighting, which the prior single-source (nina-only) backlog could not do. SERP weakness for the three net-new top topics was grounded with live Firecrawl searches (NL intent). Existing topics retain their previously live-grounded SERP notes. 'registry_status' flags overlap with outputs/content-registry.yaml so net-new angles are prioritized over already-drafted/planned topics. 'validation' on new topics records how many of the four new interviews support the angle. REVIVED-TOPICS PASS (2026-05-30): regenerated briefs for five user-requested revived topics against the 5-interview corpus (how-to-practice-dutch-speaking-alone, life-without-dutch-netherlands, passed-inburgering-cant-speak-dutch, shame-cycle-speaking-dutch, who-is-dutchwithjoost-not-for); the sixth, dutch-partner-cant-teach-dutch, already had a current brief. passed-inburgering is differentiated from the drafted why-cant-speak article and flagged for human review. PRODUCT-EXPLAINER PASS (2026-06-02): added one user-requested topic, how-does-dutchwithjoost-work (score 8.35), the canonical product 'how it works' walkthrough with screenshots. It is the product-tour sibling to the methodology pillar (what-is-production-based-language-learning) and the BOFU buyer guide (inburgering-course-that-teaches-speaking) and links out to both rather than repeating them. No Firecrawl run (branded query, uncontested). Brief carries two hard guardrails: screenshots cannot be fabricated, and product facts must be verified against the live product because the legacy messaging.md is gone. COMMUNITY-LISTENING PASS (2026-06-11): added 5 net-new topics from Facebook inburgering/learning-Dutch group listening (facebook-groups-inburgering insights). Deduped against all 45 existing topics + 22 briefs first; skipped the understand-but-cant-speak, practice-alone, and busy-job-plus-kids angles as already covered (the new mom-post evidence should ENRICH the pending learn-dutch-as-a-busy-parent / learn-dutch-irregular-schedule briefs, not get a duplicate). Net-new gaps: the speaking-exam FAILURE-recovery angle (repeated 5s, retake loop -- distinct from the first-timer inburgering-speaking-test-guide), a beginner 'where to start' roadmap, a vocabulary 'how many words' piece, and two lower-priority deviants (skip-exam-renew-residency, speaking-exam exemption). SERP weakness is estimated, not Firecrawl-grounded -- verify before writing. The residency-renewal topic carries a strategic-care flag: do NOT talk readers out of the exam; reframe to 'Dutch fits a busy life' per the master Where-We-Lose note.

cross_call_frequency.production-gap-understand-but-cant-speak: 5/5 calls (nina, alysa-implied, anna, molly, monika) + heavy web evidence. Dominant, multi-source-validated theme.
cross_call_frequency.career-jobs-blocked-without-dutch: 5/5 calls. Universal motivation: Anna (denied jobs, silent exclusion), Molly (no business future), Monika (Dutch clients), Alysa (jobs require Dutch), Nina (career ceiling). Strongest under-served topic.
cross_call_frequency.self-blame-im-bad-at-languages: 3/4 new calls (anna, molly, monika) explicitly self-blamed; Nina named it as a pattern. Distinct from the shame-in-front-of-others cycle.
cross_call_frequency.duolingo-taught-useless-phrases: 3/4 new calls (anna 'green apple', molly, monika 'my elephant is green'). No course owns the rebuttal.
cross_call_frequency.classroom-intensive-friction: 4/4 new calls: can't ask in a group of 12 (molly), level-placement failure (alysa), wrong-level/condescending content (monika), class-of-12 attention (nina).
cross_call_frequency.dutch-people-switch-to-english: 2/4 new (molly, monika) + web. SERP already owned by DutchReview + Dutch-online, so lower priority than frequency alone suggests.
cross_call_frequency.schedule-life-constraints: 4/4 new: childcare/pregnancy (alysa), newborn (anna), moving/driving-licence (monika), irregular founder hours (nina). Parent sub-angle is net-new.
cross_call_frequency.subscription-resentment: 1/4 new (anna, 3 unused subscriptions) + web. Validates one-time-pricing positioning; folds into comparison content rather than a standalone topic.

big5_audit.framework: The Big 5 (Marcus Sheridan / Endless Customers): Cost & Price, Problems, Versus & Comparisons, Reviews, Best in Class. Source: impactplus.com/learn/the-big-5-content-topics-for-business. Audited the existing 33-topic backlog + 19 briefs against the 5 high-intent buyer-research topics.

coverage.cost_and_price: PARTIAL. Market-level cost is covered (inburgering-course-cost, inburgering-course-worth-it, affordable-dutch-course-online), but the keystone Big 5 cost article -- our OWN transparent price article -- is MISSING. Marcus calls this the single most important article a business can write.
coverage.problems: STRONG. ~10 problem/symptom topics already cover the funnel (passed-inburgering-cant-speak, partner-cant-teach, not-bad-at-languages, shame-cycle, switch-to-english, pronunciation-hard, work-visa-anxiety, leaving-NL, life-without-dutch). No urgent gap.
coverage.versus_and_comparisons: GOOD. self-paced-vs-classroom, tutor-vs-self-paced, duolingo, dutchwithjoost-vs-inburgeringonline. Gaps: only one us-vs-named-competitor, and no competitor-vs-competitor piece (Marcus recommends writing these even when you are not in the matchup).
coverage.reviews: MODERATE. Three competitor reviews exist (inburgeringonline, taalthuis, uva-talen) but the two highest-traffic players are uncovered: Koentact (classroom market leader) and Inburgering.org (cheapest self-paced, rising challenger).
coverage.best_in_class: THIN. Only best-dutch-course-a2-inburgering and self-study-dutch-a2. Missing segmented best-of lists (best app to learn Dutch, best course for working professionals) -- and these lists are what now feed AI recommendations.

big5_audit.guardrails: Review and cost-gap topics must follow project content rules: never cite competitor billing/Trustpilot/subscription complaints (one-time-vs-subscription cost contrast is BOFU-only, never a billing attack); never state we are not DUO-approved (frame as 'check your track first'); compute any competitor price from actual published plan prices, not loose ranges.

ai_search_lens.mode: additive / non-destructive -- existing 41 topic objects and their priority_score values are unchanged. This block layers an AI-search (GEO/AEO) classification on top and records the four appended AI-search-native topics.
ai_search_lens.trigger: Ahrefs CMO LinkedIn post: 14 studies across 1B+ data points on AI search optimization. Applied here as a FORMAT + INTENT re-weighting of Dutch-learning content -- NOT as new topics about AI search itself (wrong audience for this brand).

source_findings_applied.listicle_dominance: 'Best X' listicles are the single most-cited content format by ChatGPT (43.8% of cited page types) -> elevate listicle/best-of and review formats as citation magnets (format_priority: high).
source_findings_applied.influenceable_surface: Only 32.3% of ChatGPT citations are influenceable (educational pages, reviews, news, blogs); 67% are Wikipedia/homepages/app stores -> concentrate effort on educational explainers and honest reviews, the surfaces we can actually win.
source_findings_applied.separate_discovery_layer: 28.3% of ChatGPT's most-cited pages have zero Google organic visibility -> AI is a separate discovery layer, so deep long-tail pain pages can be cited even when they would never rank. mini_volume is de-emphasized for ai-citation plays (existing scores left intact per the non-destructive choice; treat low mini_volume as a weaker negative for citation plays).
source_findings_applied.retrieved_vs_cited: ChatGPT cites only ~50% of the URLs it retrieves -> citation-worthiness (self-contained, quotable, named-entity answers) decides inclusion. Operationalized in each brief's aeo_directives.
source_findings_applied.schema_noise: Schema markup had ~zero measurable impact on AI citations -> do NOT invest in schema for AI visibility. FAQ blocks are kept only for human readers and featured snippets.
source_findings_applied.youtube_correlation: YouTube mentions had the highest correlation (0.737) with AI brand visibility of every factor studied, beating backlinks/DR/page count -> the single biggest lever for Joost. Each brief now carries a youtube_companion directive.
source_findings_applied.aio_click_loss: AI Overviews cut clicks to the #1 result by 58% (up from 34.5% ten months earlier) -> on informational queries, being the cited/named source beats ranking #1.
source_findings_applied.informational_only_aio: 99.9% of AI Overviews appear on informational-intent queries; transactional/navigational/branded queries are almost entirely AIO-free (shopping only 3.2%) -> barbell strategy via play_type below.
source_findings_applied.mode_vs_aio_divergence: Google AI Mode and AI Overviews reach the same conclusion 86% of the time but overlap only 13.7% on citations -> breadth of quality cluster content beats over-optimizing a single page.
source_findings_applied.aio_volatility: AIO reshuffles sources every ~2.15 days while semantic similarity stays 0.95 -> keep entity/definition language consistent across the whole cluster so any day's pull can land on us.

tag_legend.play_type: click-capture = AIO-safe branded/comparison/navigational intent where ranking still wins the click; ai-citation = informational intent that AI will summarize, so the win condition is being cited and named (expect lower CTR).
tag_legend.format_priority: high = listicle/best-of or review (the two formats ChatGPT cites most); standard = all other formats.
tag_legend.youtube_companion: true = pair the article with a Joost video and seek YouTube mentions of the brand -- the highest-correlation AI-visibility lever.

recommended_next_actions: Ship the listicle/best-of + review cluster first -- these are the formats ChatGPT cites most (best-dutch-courses-speak, best-inburgering-courses-2026, best-dutch-course-a2-inburgering, best-app-to-learn-dutch, the competitor reviews). format_priority: high.
Add a Joost YouTube companion to every format_priority/youtube_companion topic; YouTube presence is the highest-correlation AI brand-visibility lever (0.737).
On ai-citation (informational) topics, make sure DutchwithJoost is NAMED with one quotable, self-contained sentence so an AI summary can cite it -- ranking matters less than being the named source.
Keep entity and definition language identical across the cluster (e.g. 'production-based learning', 'learn by using the vocabulary') so the daily AIO reshuffle keeps landing on consistent phrasing.
Do not spend cycles on schema markup for AI visibility; it showed ~zero effect. Keep FAQ blocks for humans and featured snippets only.

topics.title: Why You Keep Failing the Inburgering Speaking Exam (and How to Break the Cycle)
topics.primary_keyword: why do I keep failing the inburgering speaking exam
topics.persona: Repeat speaking-exam failure stuck in the production gap (Pattern A Partner Visa, Pattern D Long-term Resident) and voluntary naturalisation seekers
topics.registry_status: net-new (distinct from inburgering-speaking-test-guide, which is a first-timer how-the-exam-works guide; this is failure-recovery for people who have already failed repeatedly)
topics.serp_note: Estimated weak/generic SERP -- the failure-specific long-tail ('got a 5', repeated retakes) is underserved vs the saturated 'speaking exam tips' head term. Firecrawl not run; verify before writing.
topics.validation: Facebook community listening (2026-06): multiple members report getting a 5 repeatedly (3-6x), 10-week result waits, one-attempt-at-a-time registration, and the 'short sentences to avoid mistakes' tactic.

topics.validation: Facebook listening: the 'I don't know where to start' post drew a wave of 'same situation' replies; peers recommend reading first, then listening and KNM, with writing and speaking last.

topics.primary_keyword: shame speaking Dutch as an expat
topics.persona: Pattern A / Pattern C; shame-cycle barrier
topics.registry_status: planned (canonical shame/fear-of-speaking target; why-cant-speak article links here; brief shame-cycle-speaking-dutch_brief.json). Scope: the SOCIAL shame cycle (being watched + switch-to-English), distinct from the self-blame piece not-bad-at-dutch-its-the-method.
topics.serp_note: Very weak SERP for the shame framing; emotionally resonant top-of-funnel angle. New intelligence broadens the 'you're not alone' validation across four interviews.

topics.title: You Can't Get a Job in the Netherlands Without Dutch: What to Do About It
topics.primary_keyword: can't get a job in the Netherlands without Dutch
topics.persona: Cross-persona; Career motivation (universal across all five interviews)
topics.registry_status: planned (dutch-for-career-netherlands)
topics.serp_note: Estimated: expat-portal generic advice (IamExpat, Undutchables, relocation blogs) and Reddit dominate; none connect job access to a speaking-first learning path. Differentiator fit is indirect (DWJ gets you speaking, which unlocks the career), but business priority is the highest in the backlog.
topics.validation: 5/5 calls. The most consistently cited motivation across every interview: job denial (Anna), no business future (Molly), Dutch clients (Monika), jobs requiring Dutch (Alysa), career ceiling and layoff-first (Nina).

topics.title: What Is Production-Based Language Learning?
topics.primary_keyword: production based language learning
topics.persona: Production Gap + Prior Course Skepticism; solution-aware, esp. Pattern C (Nina) and Pattern D
topics.registry_status: planned (existing content-registry entry; committed internal-link target from inburgering-course-that-teaches-speaking and why-cant-speak-dutch-after-studying). Pillar/methodology page the cluster links into.
topics.serp_note: Verified 2026-06-01 (firecrawl): SERP is academic (PubMed, Wikipedia, ResearchGate, ScienceDirect) and teacher-focused, and is partly hijacked by 'project-based language learning (PBLL)', a different concept. No learner-facing piece applies production-based learning to Dutch/inburgering, so weak for our niche (7). Head term is academic and ambiguous; lean on variants like 'why memorizing Dutch doesn't stick'. PubMed 'Production Practice During Language Learning Improves...' is a citable proof source.
topics.validation: Production gap is the most-cited cross-call pain (Anna, Molly, Monika, Nina). Nina: 'I finished it. But I didn't feel like I retained anything.'

topics.title: Is an Inburgering Course Worth It? An Honest Cost-Benefit Look
topics.primary_keyword: is an inburgering course worth it
topics.persona: Cross-persona course shopper; Prior Course Skepticism + cost-conscious (Anna, Nina, Monika)
topics.registry_status: planned (existing content-registry entry; committed internal-link target from who-is-dutchwithjoost-not-for and why-cant-speak-dutch-after-studying). BOFU.
topics.serp_note: Verified 2026-06-01 (firecrawl): serp_weakness raised 7->8. Top result is the exact Reddit thread; SERP is UGC (Reddit, Facebook), Trustpilot reviews, personal anecdotes (Medium, YouTube), and vendor self-assessment (dutchfirst.nl). No neutral cost-benefit editorial. Strong wedge confirmed. Guardrail: Trustpilot ranks #2/#9 but we deliberately do not cite billing/review complaints.
topics.validation: Anna resents subscriptions she can't use or cancel; Nina spent ~EUR700 across an A1 (EUR300) and A2 (EUR390) course and retained little. Value uncertainty is documented and cross-call.

topics.title: How Much Does an Inburgering Course Cost? Full Price Breakdown
topics.primary_keyword: inburgering course cost
topics.persona: Cost-conscious + Schedule Constraints; Pattern A (Alysa), Monika
topics.registry_status: planned (existing content-registry entry; committed internal-link target from inburgering-course-that-teaches-speaking). BOFU.
topics.serp_note: Verified 2026-06-01 (firecrawl): more contested than estimated (serp_weakness held at 6). Official sources rank strongly (inburgeren.nl, government.nl own the funding/loan answer) plus vendor pricing pages (inburgering.org, nlcompass, languagecorner, dutchready). Gap: no neutral total-cost-to-A2-by-format breakdown. Link out to government.nl for the funded route rather than compete on it.
topics.validation: Alysa: private tutoring too expensive; Monika: private tutoring out of budget; Alysa: one-shot government funding raises the stakes on getting it right.

topics.title: The Inburgering Speaking Exam: A Complete Guide
topics.primary_keyword: inburgering speaking exam
topics.persona: Inburgering-bound; Production Gap + speaking-exam anxiety; cross-persona
topics.registry_status: planned (existing content-registry entry; committed internal-link target from inburgering-course-that-teaches-speaking).
topics.serp_note: Verified 2026-06-01 (firecrawl), serp_weakness held at 6. Official (inburgeren.nl), YouTube practice videos, DutchReview, and iamexpat dominate. NEW A2 speaking format since 1 March 2025 (confirmed via inburgeren.nl + iamexpat): multiple-choice removed, now 16 spoken exercises (was 24), ~35 min. Brief must cover the change; verify exact structure on inburgeren.nl. Competitor to beat: iamexpat.nl.
topics.validation: Web research Pain Point 1 (Speaking Exam Anxiety): 'stepped nervously into DUO exam halls'; 'tests timed responses to prompts.' Nina: 'People don't even expect that at A2 you can have a conversation.'

topics.title: A2 vs B1 for Inburgering: Which Level Do You Need?
topics.primary_keyword: A2 vs B1 inburgering
topics.persona: Pattern B (work visa/career, may need B1), Pattern D (integration focus)
topics.registry_status: planned (existing content-registry entry; committed internal-link target from who-is-dutchwithjoost-not-for).
topics.serp_note: Verified 2026-06-01 (firecrawl): serp_weakness lowered 7->5 (contested). Multiple direct 'A2 or B1' pages rank (inburgering.app, nlcompass, dutch-online, DutchReview) plus Reddit/YouTube. Key determinant surfaced: the law plus 2021-Act leerroute decides your level (2013 Act = A2; 2021 Act B1/education tracks = B1; self-reliance Z track = A1, A2 optional), per strategy/factual-accuracy-checklist.md. Differentiate on that clarity plus decision-orientation. Competitors to beat: inburgering.app, nlcompass.
topics.validation: Alysa: level-placement failure, programs don't know where to put her. JTBD: 'Get placed at the correct level.' Level confusion is a documented pain.

topics.title: Best Dutch Courses for Expats Who Actually Want to Speak (2026)
topics.primary_keyword: best Dutch course to actually speak
topics.persona: Solution-aware cross-persona shopper who has decided memorization/recognition courses failed them and wants production (Pattern A Partner+Family, Pattern C Work Visa full stack, Pattern D long-term resident)
topics.registry_status: net-new (AI-search-native, 2026-06-03). Goal-based best-of list ('to actually speak') sitting above the exam-prep-focused best-dutch-course-a2-inburgering; links to it rather than repeating it.
topics.serp_note: Listicle is the single most-cited ChatGPT format (43.8%). Build a genuinely useful roundup organized by HOW each option builds speaking (self-paced production-based, 1:1 tutor, classroom social, conversation/AI apps, taalcafe, self-paced exam-prep) -- useful even if the reader picks a competitor (advice-section rule). DutchwithJoost is one honest entry, placed as the self-paced production option. Informational intent -> AI will summarize, so name the brand in one quotable sentence.
topics.ai_search_note: play_type ai-citation, format_priority high, youtube_companion true. Pair with a Joost 'what actually builds speaking' video. Win condition is being the cited+named source, not the #1 click.
topics.validation: Production gap is the dominant cross-call pain (Anna, Molly, Monika, Nina). Molly was shown the product and wanted to try lesson 1; Nina: other courses are 'a cheat sheet to the exam'.

topics.title: Best Inburgering Courses in 2026 (Honestly Compared)
topics.primary_keyword: best inburgering course 2026
topics.persona: BOFU course shopper actively comparing inburgering exam-prep options before buying (cross-persona)
topics.registry_status: net-new (AI-search-native, 2026-06-03). Best-of + review hybrid covering the real inburgering exam-prep field; complements the individual reviews and vs pages and links to them.
topics.serp_note: Listicle format ChatGPT cites most (#1) AND commercial-comparison intent that is largely AIO-free on Google (#8) -- so it is both a ChatGPT citation magnet and a Google click-capture page. Organize by buyer goal (cheapest, most recognized exam prep, to actually speak, social classroom). GUARDRAILS: compute every price from published plans; one-time-vs-subscription is value framing only, never a billing/Trustpilot attack; frame DUO funding as 'check your track and funding route first' (funded route needs a Blik op Werk certified school) -- never state we are not DUO-approved.
topics.ai_search_note: play_type click-capture, format_priority high, youtube_companion true. Answer-first verdict-by-use-case paragraph is the citable unit.
topics.validation: 'Are those EUR1000 inburgering courses actually worth it? Or scam?' (Reddit) shows the comparison demand; Anna's subscription resentment and Nina's ~EUR700 spent across two courses validate value-clarity intent.

topics.title: The Best Ways to Practice Speaking Dutch (When You Have No One to Talk To)
topics.primary_keyword: best ways to practice speaking Dutch

topics.serp_note: Listicle citation magnet, informational/TOFU-MOFU. Honest method roundup (tutors, taalcafes, language exchanges, conversation/AI apps, talking to yourself, self-paced production practice) with the trade-offs of each. Plants the production-based method and brand as the always-available, judgment-free, level-matched option.
topics.ai_search_note: play_type ai-citation, format_priority high, youtube_companion true. Strong fit for a Joost demo video of level-matched speaking practice.
topics.validation: Documented access-to-practice pain: people post looking for speaking partners; Molly: 'Sorry my Dutch is not good, can we do English?'; the Dutch Partner Problem (level mismatch).

topics.title: Best Free Ways to Learn Dutch (and Where Free Stops Working)
topics.primary_keyword: best free way to learn Dutch
topics.persona: Early-stage TOFU searcher exploring the field before spending money
topics.registry_status: net-new (AI-search-native, 2026-06-03). High-reach TOFU citation magnet; bridges to the paid/production cluster and the Duolingo teardown.
topics.serp_note: Listicle citation magnet at the top of funnel (free resources: Duolingo, YouTube, learndutch.org, free taalcafes, library resources, DUO practice). Honest about the ceiling: free tools build recognition and vocabulary but stall at production. Bridges to paid production-based learning as the step up when free stops working.
topics.ai_search_note: play_type ai-citation, format_priority high, youtube_companion true. High-volume TOFU surface for brand mentions; name the brand once as the paid step-up.
topics.validation: duolingo-taught-useless-phrases in 3/4 new calls ('my elephant is green'); free-resource preference documented in r/learndutch.
